title: Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) Reports Promising Phase 3 Results for Lonvo-z
text: Intellia Therapeutics reported promising Phase 3 results for lonvo-z in hereditary angioedema. The HAELO trial demonstrated that treatment with lonvo-z resulted in an 87% reduction in hereditary angioedema attacks compared with placebo. 62% of lonvo-z patients achieved attack-free status throughout the six-month efficacy evaluation period, versus 11% of placebo patients. Only mild to moderate adverse events were reported in the active treatment arm with no serious safety concerns. The trial enrolled 80 total patients, with 52 receiving lonvo-z and 28 receiving placebo. Approximately 49% of enrolled patients were from the United States, and 71% were on long-term prophylaxis therapy at study entry. Intellia initiated a rolling BLA submission with the FDA, expecting to complete the BLA in the second half of 2026 with potential U.S. launch in the first half of 2027.
